name: Estadio Azteca
blurb: Hosts the opener on June 11 — the first stadium ever to stage three World Cup opening matches. A cauldron at 2,240m altitude where Pelé and Maradona both lifted the trophy.

name: MetLife Stadium
blurb: Stages the FINAL on July 19. Just across the Hudson from Manhattan, this is the tournament's grand finale on the biggest stage in world sport.

name: AT&T Stadium
blurb: A colossal, climate-controlled Texas cathedral in Arlington with the largest video board in the game — set to host a string of knockout clashes.

name: Arrowhead Stadium
blurb: Home to the loudest recorded crowd in sport. The heartland's BBQ capital brings a wall of Midwestern noise to the World Cup.

name: NRG Stadium
blurb: A retractable-roof, air-conditioned dome on the Gulf Coast — Texas' most diverse, international city, ready to beat the summer heat.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
blurb: The South's biggest hub, with a futuristic petal-roof arena and one of the most fervent soccer fanbases in the United States.

name: SoFi Stadium
blurb: A $5-billion architectural marvel in Inglewood — Hollywood glamour meets cutting-edge design on the Pacific coast.

name: Lumen Field
blurb: Pacific Northwest soccer heartland, where Sounders supporters set the bar for atmosphere in North American football.

name: Lincoln Financial Field
blurb: The birthplace of American independence brings its famously passionate sports crowds to the world's game.

name: Levi's Stadium
blurb: Set in Santa Clara at the heart of Silicon Valley — the tech coast's high-tech, sustainable venue.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
blurb: Tropical, bilingual and football-mad — the gateway between the USA and Latin America, with a shaded canopy over the stands.

name: Gillette Stadium
blurb: New England's home in Foxborough — historic, college-town energy a short hop from one of America's oldest cities.

name: BC Place
blurb: Where the mountains meet the ocean — a downtown, retractable-roof stadium in Canada's stunning Pacific gateway.

name: Estadio BBVA
blurb: 'El Gigante de Acero' sits beneath the iconic Cerro de la Silla in Mexico's industrial powerhouse of the north.

name: Estadio Akron
blurb: The birthplace of mariachi and tequila, in the heart of Mexican football — home of legendary club Chivas.

name: BMO Field
blurb: Canada's largest, most multicultural city — a lakeside ground expanded specially to welcome the world.
